title: Dryopteris paleaceolobata
intro: Dryopteris paleaceolobata, also known as Paleaceolobed Wood Fern or Paleaceolobed Male Fern, is a species of fern native to Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its use in traditional medicine to alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris paleaceolobata include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often made into preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
